mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically lifting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate drops below 8.0 percent, labor market slack dissipates, triggering wage growth expectations and boosting consumer spending demand, which drives positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day forward returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 8.0% over a 5-year test window.

mechanism_narrative: Strong industrial production combined with rising housing starts signals synchronized real-economy expansion, compressing risk premiums and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields a negative directional accuracy below 50% over any 3-year out-of-sample test, indicating the interaction does not predict equity returns.

mechanism_narrative: In a disinflationary environment where CPIAUCSL growth decelerates while FEDFUNDS remains anchored near zero, real yields fall and equity risk premiums compress, driving positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns turn negative when CPIAUCSL month-over-month growth exceeds 0.4% while FEDFUNDS stays below 0.5%, falsifying the disinflationary premium compression claim.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real GDP directly reflects aggregate economic activity, which translates into higher corporate revenue expectations and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days following each release.
failure_condition: Rank IC of GDPC1 revisions against 5-day forward SPX returns remains statistically indistinguishable from zero (p > 0.10) across all post-2005 quarters.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the industrial production index exceeding 0.5 percent signals supply-side capacity utilization tightening, which historically precedes positive earnings revisions and drives S&P 500 returns higher over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward SPX returns are negative in more than 60% of months where INDPRO exceeds the 0.5% monthly growth threshold over a 10-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in nonfarm payrolls and rising private residential investment indicates sustained household balance sheet repair, which fuels durable goods demand and generates positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to produce a positive directional accuracy above 52% over any rolling 4-year window, indicating the labor-consumption linkage does not drive equity valuations.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of rapid housing market recovery, rising housing starts amplify consumer wealth effects and construction-related corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward SPX returns show no significant positive skew when HOUST exceeds its 12-month moving average, falsifying the housing wealth effect transmission channel.
